caveats: a: The occupation table is four-digit NAICS 311900, not six-digit 311991.
a: The estimate cannot observe the baseline of conventional automation, robotics, and software already in use.
a: Waste and throughput improvements may create economic value without substituting wage hours and therefore are not counted in a.
rho: BTOS measures broad business-function use, including drafting and search that may not generate labor savings.
rho: FDA requirements do not prohibit AI but require monitoring, verification, corrective action, and records around safety-critical controls.
rho: Rapidly changing daily menus and short data histories can make models fragile.
e: No public dataset reports the eligible firm share at six digits.
e: FreshRealm is a prominent scaled platform, not a representative LMM firm.
e: Private-label sales qualify only when they reflect a repeat outsourced operating capability for an external customer.
s5: One strategic asset sale does not measure annual transfer probability.
s5: The owner-age evidence is old, broad, and limited to linked single-unit firms.
s5: Internal reorganizations, closures, and asset liquidations without a transferable operating business are excluded.
q: The fixed-price contract evidence is older and from value-added salads rather than the entire six-digit industry.
q: Retention varies between differentiated custom meal programs and easily rebid fresh-cut commodities.
q: The estimate excludes volume loss, waste benefits, and implementation difficulty.
d5: Convenience-food research spans frozen, shelf-stable, restaurant, and other products outside 311991.
d5: Real foodservice spending does not isolate constant-quality perishable prepared-food units.
d5: FreshRealm's capacity and reach are company-provided figures and may represent share shift rather than category growth.
o: No source directly measures operator-required year-five quantity.
o: Insourcing changes the operator's identity and may remove volume from the lens even though physical work persists.
o: Automation could sharply reduce staffing without eliminating the accountable manufacturing entity.

envelope.interval_scope: research-input sensitivity with dataset anchors (l, n) held fixed

lens.customer_and_revenue_model: Recurring business-to-business unit sales and manufacturing programs, often customer-specific or private label, with high-frequency ordering, strict freshness and service windows, retailer or foodservice specifications, and revenue tied to cases, units, or meals delivered.
lens.excluded_activities: Own-brand-only food companies without recurring outsourced production, restaurant or grocery in-store kitchens classified outside manufacturing, captive commissaries, shelf-stable or frozen products outside 311991, shells, and non-control financing vehicles.
lens.included_activities: Lower-middle-market U.S. manufacturers repeatedly producing short-shelf-life prepared meals, salads, sandwiches, fresh pizza and pasta, peeled or cut vegetables, and similar refrigerated foods for external retailers, brands, meal platforms, institutions, or foodservice customers under private-label, co-packing, contract-production, or customized supply relationships.

a.bridge: BLS OEWS for broader NAICS 311900 reports 43.62% of employment in production, 20.91% in transportation/material moving, 7.15% in office support, 6.04% in maintenance, 5.69% in management, and 3.64% in business/financial operations. ILO research finds GenAI exposure highest in clerical and digitized work. I apply high task exposure to planning, order processing, procurement, commercial, documentation, and analytical work; moderate exposure to management, quality-record, and maintenance-diagnostic tasks; and low exposure to food preparation, sanitation, packing, and material movement, then adjust for higher wages in the exposed roles. Other Food Manufacturing includes nonperishable products and may understate the hand assembly and cold-chain intensity of 311991, potentially biasing exposure upward.
caveats: The occupation table is four-digit NAICS 311900, not six-digit 311991.
The estimate cannot observe the baseline of conventional automation, robotics, and software already in use.
Waste and throughput improvements may create economic value without substituting wage hours and therefore are not counted in a.
a.change_evidence: A 311991 wage-by-SOC file combined with time-and-motion studies at meal, sandwich, salad, pasta, and fresh-cut produce plants, explicitly identifying already-automated tasks and paid hours that validated AI workflows can remove or avoid.
a.rationale: AI can address demand forecasting, shelf-life and waste analysis, labor scheduling, production planning, order and specification handling, customer communication, food-safety record review, vision inspection, and maintenance triage. Most payroll still supports physical ingredient handling, preparation, assembly, packaging, cleaning, cold-chain movement, and accountable release that software cannot directly substitute.

d5.bridge: USDA finds that U.S. demand for convenience foods grew over four decades, and USDA's food-expenditure series shows inflation-adjusted foodservice sales reaching $1.41 trillion in 2025 from $818 billion in 1997. FreshRealm reports built capacity of 15 million meals weekly and ongoing partnerships across brands and retailers. I translate structural convenience demand, broad away-from-home growth, modest population growth, and current industry investment into mid-single-digit five-year constant-quality quantity growth. Historic spending and a vendor's capacity are not current 311991 physical-demand measures and can overstate growth if capacity displaces incumbents rather than expands the market.
caveats: Convenience-food research spans frozen, shelf-stable, restaurant, and other products outside 311991.
Real foodservice spending does not isolate constant-quality perishable prepared-food units.
FreshRealm's capacity and reach are company-provided figures and may represent share shift rather than category growth.
d5.change_evidence: Five-year Circana and NielsenIQ unit-volume data for fresh prepared meals, salads, sandwiches, fresh pasta and cut produce, combined with Technomic foodservice volumes, retailer perimeter forecasts, and target-customer contracted case pipelines normalized for quality and mix.
d5.rationale: Time-saving fresh meals, salads, sandwiches, cut produce, and meal components serve durable convenience needs across grocery, foodservice, travel, healthcare, and direct-to-consumer channels. Population and channel outsourcing support growth, while price sensitivity, food waste, in-store preparation, restaurant cyclicality, and demand forecasting errors create downside.

e.bridge: FreshRealm explicitly operates a fresh-food service platform providing product development, ingredient manufacturing, production, fulfillment, and customized programs for brands and retailers, while Calavo's former Fresh Cut business sold sandwiches, salads, parfaits, fresh-cut produce, and snacks through store-brand and private-label programs. These examples prove the recurring outsourced model but do not measure its share among 311991 LMM firms. I infer a substantial eligible share, then discount branded manufacturers, captive commissaries, and firms whose revenue is primarily their own consumer proposition. Large visible platforms may overrepresent outsourcing relative to the long tail, biasing the estimate upward.
caveats: No public dataset reports the eligible firm share at six digits.
FreshRealm is a prominent scaled platform, not a representative LMM firm.
Private-label sales qualify only when they reflect a repeat outsourced operating capability for an external customer.
e.change_evidence: A revenue-model census of the estimated 240 LMM firms classifying customer-owned/private-label, custom contract, co-pack, own-brand, and captive revenue, together with contract recurrence, customer tenure, and owner-dependence data.
e.rationale: Retailers, brands, meal platforms, institutions, and foodservice customers outsource perishable product development and production because local facilities, food-safety systems, labor, procurement, and cold-chain delivery are difficult to replicate. Still, many fresh-food manufacturers mainly sell proprietary brands and do not meet the service lens.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 311991 ($3,712,502,251) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 311991 ($25,925,157,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

n.detail: margin 0.1525 (margins.json[31]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Food Processing EBITDA/Sales 15.25% (dominant subsector of NAICS 31)); revenue/employee $349,118 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 311991); class <5: 297 firms, avg revenue/firm $633,768 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $96,650 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 126 firms, avg revenue/firm $2,227,246 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $339,655 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 118 firms, avg revenue/firm $2,969,678 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $452,876 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 165 firms, avg revenue/firm $9,695,612 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,478,581 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 75 firms, avg revenue/firm $63,285,560 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $9,651,048 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 71 firms, avg revenue/firm $264,220,845 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $40,293,679 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 240

caveats: No source directly measures operator-required year-five quantity.
Insourcing changes the operator's identity and may remove volume from the lens even though physical work persists.
Automation could sharply reduce staffing without eliminating the accountable manufacturing entity.
o.change_evidence: Customer interviews on insourcing and automated-commissary plans, retailer robotics pilots, and evidence that software or equipment providers will assume environmental-pathogen, product-release, recall, and cold-chain accountability rather than merely supply tools.
o.rationale: The service basket requires physical sourcing, preparation, assembly, cooking where applicable, packaging, chilling, environmental monitoring, release, and time-sensitive delivery. Software may plan and control these activities, but FDA's Listeria framework requires plant operators to prevent contamination, verify controls, act on environmental findings, and maintain records. Some outsourced quantity can move into retailer or restaurant kitchens, but an accountable operator still performs the work.

q.bridge: A public filing for a large value-added salad supplier describes annual and multi-year fixed-price-per-case retail contracts with fuel-index surcharges, while Calavo reports private-label programs, significant top-customer concentration, and exposure to order reduction or cancellation. These are observed contract and buyer-power mechanisms in adjacent 311991 activities, not a direct measurement of retained AI benefit. I allow retention inside fixed-price periods and for service or freshness differentiation, then reduce it for retailer procurement, contract renewal, competitive bids, customer consolidation, and transparent operating benchmarks. Large public suppliers may face more concentrated buyers than niche LMM firms, potentially biasing retention downward.
caveats: The fixed-price contract evidence is older and from value-added salads rather than the entire six-digit industry.
Retention varies between differentiated custom meal programs and easily rebid fresh-cut commodities.
The estimate excludes volume loss, waste benefits, and implementation difficulty.
q.change_evidence: Target-level contracts and renewal histories showing pricing term, termination, volume commitment, spoilage and service penalties, cost indices, open-book clauses, productivity sharing, formula ownership, and gross-margin bridges before and after automation.
q.rationale: Fixed case prices and the value of dependable short-shelf-life execution can protect gains temporarily. Over five years, sophisticated retail and foodservice customers can reprice, rebid, require productivity commitments, or capture benefits through service expectations, especially where recipes and labels are customer-owned.

rho.bridge: Census BTOS research reports AI use by 18% of firms in late 2025/early 2026, 32% employment-weighted, but 66% of users employed AI only to augment tasks and only 2% reported AI-related employment decreases. I translate that economy-wide adoption depth into a five-year implemented share, allowing meaningful deployment in planning and administrative workflows while discounting small-firm data maturity, short shelf-life operational volatility, validation requirements, customer approvals, and integration with plant systems. Economy-wide and large-employer adoption likely overstates LMM prepared-food implementation capacity.
caveats: BTOS measures broad business-function use, including drafting and search that may not generate labor savings.
FDA requirements do not prohibit AI but require monitoring, verification, corrective action, and records around safety-critical controls.
Rapidly changing daily menus and short data histories can make models fragile.
rho.change_evidence: A survey of 311991 plants covering current and funded AI deployments by workflow, system integration time, model-error and recall-risk controls, employee adoption, and verified labor hours eliminated or hiring avoided after twelve months.
rho.rationale: Forecasting, scheduling, order handling, document review, customer service, and some inspection can be deployed within five years. Fragmented demand, daily production resets, recipe and allergen complexity, cold-chain execution, high turnover, older equipment, and the need to validate food-safety controls prevent complete implementation of the exposed opportunity.

s5.bridge: Calavo sold its Fresh Cut prepared-food business as a going concern to an existing co-packing partner for $83 million in 2024, demonstrating transferability in the specific activity, while broad Census research reports 49.55% of linked single-unit employer-firm primary owners older than 54 in 2007. I combine observed industry transfer evidence with general succession pressure and discount for one-deal selection, stale age data, firms outside the target EBITDA band, and businesses that cannot pass food-safety, customer, lease, labor, or cold-chain diligence. The one public deal overstates repeatability, while general owner aging may understate current succession pressure.
caveats: One strategic asset sale does not measure annual transfer probability.
The owner-age evidence is old, broad, and limited to linked single-unit firms.
Internal reorganizations, closures, and asset liquidations without a transferable operating business are excluded.
s5.change_evidence: Five years of PitchBook, Axial, GF Data, BizBuySell, SBA 7(a), broker-CRM, and food-industry transaction data filtered to 311991 activities, matched to owner age, attempted sales, customer novation, and failed-diligence reasons.
s5.rationale: The estimated target cohort is large and tangible facilities, customer programs, certifications, and local distribution networks can transfer. However, customer concentration, short-term programs, leased plants, working-capital volatility, key-person culinary knowledge, labor instability, and environmental-pathogen history frequently impair sale completion.

principal_driver: Recurring customer demand for safe, fresh, convenient food creates a persistent outsourced operator role with valuable scheduling, order, quality, and waste workflows for AI.
principal_weakness: Most payroll supports physical production and cold-chain execution, while the eligible share and retained benefit rely on broad or company-specific proxies rather than six-digit measurement.
